A native macOS SwiftUI application for managing local Codex and Claude Code skills. It browses skills from standard skill folders, renders each SKILL.md with Markdown and inline reference previews, imports skills from folders or zip files, and downloads remote skills from a catalog.

Unofficial Python API, CLI, and agent skill providing programmatic access to Google NotebookLM, including features the web UI does not expose. Integrates with AI coding agents such as Claude Code and Codex and supports notebook and podcast generation workflows.

A context-compression layer for LLM applications that shrinks tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the model to reduce token usage. Ships as a Python library, a proxy, and an MCP server, aiming to cut tokens while preserving answers.

A real-time global intelligence dashboard that aggregates news and tracks geopolitical and infrastructure developments in a unified situational-awareness interface. Provides AI-powered monitoring across finance, energy, and commodity domains with web and SDK access.

Multi-agent orchestration for the Claude Code CLI, designed to coordinate teams of agents for parallel coding tasks with minimal setup. Provides workflows and a CLI to run and manage automated multi-agent execution without a steep learning curve.

An open-source, customizable design system built on React and StyleX, shipping over 150 accessible components with brand-level theming, dark mode, ready-made templates, and a CLI. Designed for use by both developers and AI agents, with no styling-library lock-in.

LLM-powered stock analysis system covering A-share, Hong Kong, US, Japanese, Korean, and Taiwanese markets. It aggregates multi-source market data and news, generates a daily decision dashboard with scores and buy and sell points, and pushes the reports to WeCom, Feishu, Telegram, Discord, Slack, and email on a schedule.

Installable library of over 1,900 reusable agentic skills for coding agents such as Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI. It provides an installer CLI, a read-only local MCP server for skill search and recommendation, specialized plugins, curated bundles, and ordered execution workflows.

Open-source platform for managing coding agents as teammates. Assign issues to agents that pick up work, write code, report blockers, and update status autonomously, group them into squads with a leader that routes work, schedule recurring autopilots, and run local or cloud runtimes from one dashboard. Works with Claude Code, Codex, and other agent CLIs.

Command-line tool that automatically downloads books from Z-Library and uploads them to Google NotebookLM in a single step. It prioritizes PDF with an EPUB-to-Markdown fallback, uses browser automation for a one-time login, and splits large files into chunks for reliable upload. It can also be used as a Claude skill.

Open-source AI desktop agent, built with Electron, that automates file management, document creation, and browser tasks locally on your machine. Bring your own API keys for OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or xAI, or run local models via Ollama. It runs locally with per-folder access control and approval for every action.

A community archive of Chinese-language deep-dive analyses and bilingual English-Chinese transcripts for 297 episodes of Lenny's Podcast, organized by guest with per-episode analysis reports, transcripts, PDF exports, and topic indexes covering product management, growth, leadership, and AI.

A collection of AI agent skills written as markdown files that give coding agents specialized marketing workflows, covering conversion optimization, copywriting, SEO, analytics, ads, email, and growth engineering. Compatible with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and other agents following the Agent Skills standard.

A cross-harness operator system for AI coding agents that packages skills, subagents, hooks, rules, and MCP configurations alongside memory persistence, continuous learning, and security scanning. Works across Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, and Gemini, installable as a plugin or via a manual installer.

An agent-agnostic React tool that adds a visual feedback toolbar to a web app, letting you click, select, or drag to annotate page elements and copy structured markdown output with CSS selectors, positions, and context so AI coding agents can locate the referenced code. Requires React 18 and a desktop browser.

A library of agent skills and plugins for Google Stitch that work through the Stitch MCP server, providing workflows to generate and edit UI designs, convert frontend code to designs, extract design systems, and build React or React Native components. Compatible with Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, and Cursor.

A cross-platform desktop GUI for yt-dlp that downloads video from YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, and 1800+ sites, with batch and playlist downloads, audio extraction, subtitle tools, signed plugins, a browser extension, and optional AI video summaries and natural-language processing via Gemini, OpenAI, or Ollama.

A command-line tool that unifies configuration for AI coding assistants such as Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor. It merges AGENTS.md, rules, commands, and skills into a shared .ai-global directory and symlinks each tool to it, so editing one file syncs settings across all tools in system or project mode.

A Chinese-localized Claude Code skill that rewrites text to remove traces of AI-generated writing. It scans for 24 documented patterns across content, grammar, style, and filler categories, then rewrites flagged passages into more natural, human-sounding prose while preserving the original meaning.

The recommendation system powering the For You feed on X. It combines in-network posts from followed accounts with out-of-network posts found by a two-tower retrieval model, then ranks them with a Grok-based transformer that predicts engagement probabilities. Ships a runnable retrieval-to-ranking inference pipeline in Rust and Python.

Browserbase's official collection of agent skills, packaged as a Claude Code plugin, for web browser automation through the browse CLI. Skills cover remote browser sessions, DevTools tracing, serverless browser functions, static web fetching and search, adversarial UI testing, and company and competitor research workflows.

OpenAI-compatible API middleware that exposes n8n agents and workflows as OpenAI models for any OpenAI-compatible client. Supports streaming and non-streaming responses, multi-model JSON configuration, session tracking, per-endpoint rate limiting, and Docker deployment with hot-reload of models.

A curated collection of specialized AI agent personalities that install into coding assistants such as Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and Gemini CLI. Each agent is a markdown-defined expert with a distinct role, workflow, and deliverables, installed through shell scripts or an accompanying desktop app.

A Python mobile automation agent that uses Qwen3-VL vision-language models to visually understand Android screens and control devices through ADB commands. Tasks are described in natural language and run from a Gradio web UI or the command line, with live screenshots and execution logs.

An autonomous financial research agent that decomposes complex questions into structured research plans, executes them against real-time market data, and validates its own results. Built in TypeScript on the Bun runtime with task planning, self-reflection, loop detection, and an optional WhatsApp interface.

A browser-based 3D building and architectural editor built with React Three Fiber and WebGPU on Next.js. Model sites, buildings, levels, walls, and furnishings with an extensible plugin system, undo/redo history, and IndexedDB persistence, then share the resulting 3D architectural projects.

An open-source library of production-ready skills, agents, and plugins for Claude Code and a dozen other AI coding tools, covering engineering, DevOps, marketing, compliance, C-level advisory, research, and productivity, with custom slash commands and reusable reference packages.

A Claude Skills-based toolkit of workflows and checklists for individual equity investors and swing traders, structuring market review, risk management, trade planning, and journaling. It is positioned as an educational decision-process aid, not a signal service or automated trading system.

An open-source, cross-platform desktop video downloader built with Electron and powered by yt-dlp, downloading from over 1000 sites with configurable output containers and RSS-based automatic background downloads. It also ships a Docker-ready web and API server.

A systematic open-source teaching project, written in Chinese, for building full-stack AI agents from scratch in Python. It progresses through prompting, LLM calls, function calling, LangChain, RAG, LangGraph, and MCP with runnable example modules paired with detailed explanatory blog posts.

An open-source, self-hosted AI trading terminal written in Go where a language model drives the strategy while the runtime clamps every order to hard risk limits it cannot override. It supports nine exchanges, autopilot execution, encrypted local credentials, and a public return leaderboard.

A reference library of production-tested Claude Code infrastructure: skills that auto-activate through hooks, a modular 500-line skill pattern with progressive disclosure, specialized agents, slash commands, and a dev-docs system. It ships a setup wizard, optional AI-powered skill classification, and cross-agent support for Codex.

A Claude Code workspace for creating long-form, SEO-optimized blog content. It provides custom commands, specialized agents, and marketing skills for researching, writing, analyzing, and optimizing articles, with Python modules for readability, keyword, and SEO quality scoring, plus Google Analytics, Search Console, and WordPress integrations.

Context Hub gives coding agents curated, versioned, language-specific documentation through the chub CLI. Agents search and fetch open markdown docs to reduce API hallucinations, attach local annotations to record gaps for future sessions, and send up or down feedback to doc authors so the content improves over time.

Cloudflare's React component library for building modern, accessible web applications. Built on Base UI, it provides design-system-compliant components that handle keyboard navigation, focus management, and ARIA attributes, supports tree-shaking via granular imports, and includes a CLI for querying component documentation.

MCPorter is a TypeScript runtime, CLI, and code-generation toolkit for the Model Context Protocol. It auto-discovers MCP servers already configured in tools like Cursor, Claude, and Codex, calls their tools from code or the command line, generates standalone CLIs, emits typed TypeScript clients, and records or replays traffic for testing.

Tracks the shareholding ratios of Taiwan's three major institutional investors across listed and OTC stocks. A Python pipeline fetches daily TWSE and TPEX data, estimates trust and dealer holdings from a baseline, computes changes over 5, 20, 60, and 120-day windows, and publishes JSON plus a static ranking dashboard to GitHub Pages.

A macOS menu bar app that displays usage limits, reset windows, credit balances, and spend for AI coding providers such as Codex, OpenAI, Claude, Cursor, Gemini, and Copilot, reusing existing provider sessions so no passwords are stored.

An open-source marketplace of 11 role-based plugins for Claude Cowork and Claude Code that bundle skills, connectors, slash commands, and sub-agents for functions such as sales, finance, legal, marketing, and data, and can be customized to a company's own tools and workflows.

A single-binary Rust CLI proxy that filters and compresses command outputs before they reach an LLM's context, reducing token consumption on common development commands by roughly 60 to 90 percent with under 10ms overhead and support for over 100 commands.

A curated open-source library of more than 10,000 community-collected prompts for Google's Nano Banana Pro AI image generation model, organized by use case with preview images and available in 16 languages alongside a companion web gallery.

A catalog of Agent Skills, which are folders of instructions, scripts, and resources that AI agents can discover to perform specific tasks, for use and distribution with OpenAI Codex; the repository is now deprecated in favor of the OpenAI Plugins repository.

MiroFish is a multi-agent AI prediction engine that extracts real-world seed information such as breaking news or financial signals and constructs a high-fidelity digital sandbox where thousands of agents with memory and personas interact, letting users simulate social evolution and forecast future trajectories.

TRELLIS.2 is a 4-billion-parameter large 3D generative model for high-fidelity image-to-3D generation. It uses a field-free sparse voxel structure called O-Voxel to reconstruct assets with complex topology, sharp features, and full PBR materials, and ships inference and training code.

Antigravity Manager is a cross-platform desktop application built with Tauri and Rust for managing multiple AI accounts and acting as a local API gateway. It converts web sessions into OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini compatible endpoints, adding one-click account switching, model routing, and quota monitoring.

A curated collection of over forty tips for using Claude Code effectively, ranging from basics to advanced workflows. It includes a customizable status-line script, guidance on running Claude Code inside a container, and a dx plugin providing skills for everyday development tasks.

Folia is a music player centered on full-screen immersive lyrics, supporting NetEase Cloud, Navidrome, and local libraries. It offers smart lyric matching, AI-generated color themes, and multiple full-screen lyric animations, with Electron desktop builds and a Node.js web version.

An AI-native application that generates editable PowerPoint presentations from prompts, outlines, documents, and custom template images. It supports conversational region-level editing, material parsing, per-page template matching, PPTX export, and project history, with Docker self-hosting and a desktop build.

An AI skill and CLI that provides design intelligence for building professional UI/UX across multiple platforms and frameworks. Its v2 design-system generator analyzes project requirements to produce tailored design systems, and it integrates with AI coding assistants such as Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

An open-source, in-process vector database from Alibaba for low-latency similarity search that embeds directly into applications. It provides dense-vector, sparse-vector, and full-text hybrid retrieval with scalar filtering and reranking, an on-disk DiskANN index, and official Go, Rust, Python, and JavaScript SDKs.

Educational, from-scratch NumPy implementations of the 30 foundational deep-learning papers on Ilya Sutskever's reading list. Each Jupyter notebook runs on synthetic data with extensive visualizations and explanations to demonstrate core concepts, and the suite covers all 30 papers for interactive learning.

A local, on-device command-line search engine for markdown notes, documentation, meeting transcripts, and knowledge bases. It combines BM25 full-text search, vector semantic search, and LLM re-ranking via node-llama-cpp with GGUF models, and exposes an MCP server for agentic workflows.

A Krita plugin that uses a local Ollama LLM such as Gemma to generate, refine, and translate image prompts, then sends them straight into the krita-ai-diffusion Stable Diffusion plugin. It runs fully offline with a dockable panel, voice input, and bilingual English and Chinese output.

Vercel's official collection of skills for AI coding agents, following the Agent Skills format. Bundled skills audit Vercel projects for cost and performance, apply React and Next.js best practices, review UI code for accessibility, and check docs against Vercel's writing guidelines.

A curated collection of prompt templates for generating presentation slides with NotebookLM and similar tools. Organized by visual style, the prompts help turn papers, notes, and transcripts into clean, structured, presentation-ready decks.

A single-binary Go command-line tool for Google Workspace with task-first commands across Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Slides, and admin. It offers stable JSON and plain output, multiple identities, read-only and agent-safety controls, and a typed MCP server for predictable automation.

Open-source Polymarket trading infrastructure and strategy reverse-engineering toolkit. It provides Java 21 microservices for automated paper and live execution, market making, and trade ingestion into ClickHouse, with a Redpanda event pipeline, a Grafana and Prometheus monitoring stack, and a quantitative research toolkit.

A lightweight meta-prompting, context-engineering, and spec-driven development system for the Claude Code coding agent. According to its README, the project has moved and now continues as GSD Core in the Open GSD repository.

A Python research project that reverse-engineers Google's SynthID image watermark using signal processing and spectral analysis. It provides watermark detection, a multi-stage removal pipeline, a spectral codebook builder, and an optional drag-and-drop desktop app, stated to be for research and educational use.

A continuously updated reference and interview-preparation guide for designing production AI systems. It covers RAG architectures, LLM engineering, agentic systems, model selection, evaluation, and observability, with case studies, whiteboard exercises, and a large question bank.

A free, open-source, self-hosted AI video platform bundling a clip generator, an AI Shorts creator with AI actors, and a YouTube studio for thumbnails and titles. It runs via Docker, integrates Gemini, fal.ai, and ElevenLabs, and can publish directly to TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube.

An automated multi-market stock monitoring and data-warehousing system covering six global markets. It scrapes market data into a SQLite database, computes rolling-trading-day indicators and 10% return-bin matrices, renders charts with Matplotlib, and emails interactive daily reports through the Resend API.

A plug-and-play Python inference library for Recursive Language Models (RLMs), which let a language model programmatically examine, decompose, and recursively call itself over near-infinite contexts. It provides an inference engine, multiple REPL sandboxes such as local, Docker, Modal, and Prime, and a reinforcement-learning training harness.

A collection of Agent Skills for context engineering and harness engineering, teaching how to curate the model's context window, design multi-agent architectures, build memory and tools, and evaluate agent behavior. It ships as a Claude Code plugin marketplace and an Open Plugins package usable across agent platforms such as Cursor and Codex.

A high-performance Graph-RAG framework written in Rust that implements the LightRAG algorithm, decomposing documents into a knowledge graph of entities and relationships for retrieval-augmented generation. It offers six query modes, a REST API, PostgreSQL storage with pgvector and Apache AGE, multiple LLM providers, and Docker-based deployment.

Provides programmatic access to Google NotebookLM through a command-line interface, a Model Context Protocol server, and AI agent skills. Users and AI assistants can manage notebooks and sources, run queries, generate audio, video, and other studio content, and share notebooks, authenticating with browser-extracted cookies.

A collaborative team workspace that brings AI agents into channels, threads, and shared documents alongside people. Built on the OpenClaw agent runtime, it offers real-time public and private channels, direct messages, one-click agent creation, file sharing, and multi-workspace isolation, with both cloud and self-hosted deployment options.

DeepTutor is an agent-native, self-hostable learning workspace that unifies tutoring, quiz generation, deep research, visualization, and mastery practice on a single agent loop. It supports multiple LLM providers, retrieval over user knowledge bases with vector backends, persistent memory, a CLI, and Docker deployment.

QuantDinger is an open-source, self-hosted AI trading platform for crypto, stocks, and forex. It combines multi-provider AI market research, Python strategy development, server-side backtesting, and paper or live execution with monitoring, keeping market data, strategy code, and broker credentials under the operator's control.

Containarium is an open-source, self-hostable runtime that gives each AI agent a persistent, SSH-reachable Linux box with per-tenant network isolation. It uses eBPF egress policy, Kubernetes or LXC backends, and GPU passthrough, and exposes its admin surface and an in-box shell over an MCP-native interface.

Its Hover is an open-source, MIT-licensed animated icon library built with React and the Motion library. It provides 186+ drop-in icon components that animate on interaction, work with Next.js and shadcn tooling, and can be installed via a shadcn CLI command or by copying the source for customization.

A Claude Code plugin for long-form web-novel writing that keeps character settings, foreshadowing, and timelines consistent across hundreds of chapters. It provides slash commands for initialization, planning, chapter drafting, review, and querying, backed by a fact-committing state system, RAG retrieval, long-term memory, and a read-only dashboard.

PDFCraft is a free, privacy-focused PDF toolkit that runs entirely in the browser using WebAssembly, so files are never uploaded to a server. It offers 80+ tools to edit, convert, merge, split, compress, and secure PDFs, plus a beta visual workflow editor for chaining operations into automated batch pipelines.

OpenViking is an open-source context database for AI agents that unifies agent memory, knowledge RAG, and skills in one store. It aims to replace fragmented, flat retrieval with structured, observable context management, addressing surging context demand and poor retrieval effectiveness in long-running agent tasks.

A Python library for fine-tuning language, vision, audio, embedding, and OCR models locally on Apple Silicon Macs using Apple's MLX framework. It exposes an Unsloth-compatible API and supports SFT, DPO, ORPO, GRPO, and other training methods, with export to HuggingFace and GGUF formats.

A full-stack TypeScript platform that automates short-drama video production, using large language models plus image and video generation to turn a script into characters, storyboards, voiceovers, and a finished video. It runs several Mastra AI agents, composes clips with FFmpeg, and can be self-hosted via Docker.

A workflow engine for Claude Code, built around a Go binary bridge, that turns Claude into a multi-model orchestrator dispatching work to Codex, Gemini, and Grok. It auto-selects among ten strategies by task type, injects state through hooks to survive context compaction, and runs quality gates with dual-model reviews.

A local, OpenAI-compatible router that connects AI coding tools such as Claude Code, Cursor, and Cline to more than 40 providers and 100+ models. It adds automatic fallback across subscription, cheap, and free tiers, tracks quota, and compresses tool-result content to reduce token usage, managed from a web dashboard.

A TypeScript tool that monitors a social-media account's Threads and Facebook posts through Apify, deduplicates new posts, and runs LLM analysis to derive contrarian stock signals that it pushes to Telegram, Discord, and LINE. It supports real-time polling, backtesting against Taiwan stock data, and builds a static signals site.

An MCP server written in Python with FastMCP that lets any MCP-compatible AI assistant query Taiwan's public legal data. It exposes eight read-only tools covering Judicial Yuan court judgments, the national regulations database of over 11,700 laws, and Constitutional Court interpretations with an offline cache.

OmniVoice Studio is an open-source, fully local alternative to ElevenLabs for voice work. It runs zero-shot voice cloning, text-to-speech, video dubbing, dictation, and audiobook creation on your own machine across many languages, bundling multiple TTS and ASR engines behind a desktop app with a FastAPI backend.

A self-hosted email client that runs entirely on Cloudflare Workers, using Email Routing for delivery, a per-mailbox Durable Object with SQLite for storage, and R2 for attachments. A built-in AI agent can read the inbox, search conversations, and draft replies, always requiring confirmation before sending.

A Claude Code plugin from OpenAI that lets you run Codex from inside Claude Code to review code or delegate tasks. It adds slash commands for read-only and adversarial code reviews, delegating work, transferring sessions, and managing background jobs, wrapping the local Codex CLI and app server.

dot-skill is a skill engine for AI agent hosts such as Claude Code and Codex that distills a person, a colleague, relationship, or public figure, into a reusable AI Skill from source material like chat logs, documents, or transcripts. The generated Skill aims to reason and speak in that person's frame and voice.

Unofficial reconstruction of the Anthropic Claude Code CLI TypeScript sources (version 2.1.88), recovered from the published npm package source maps for research purposes only. It contains the restored tools, commands, services, and plugin and skill modules, and is explicitly not the official development repository.

Claw Code is a Rust implementation of the claw command-line agent harness, a terminal-first coding assistant that connects to Anthropic, OpenAI-compatible, and local model backends such as Ollama and llama.cpp, with sessions, tools, and MCP support. It is presented as an agent-maintained exhibit.

A curated collection of DESIGN.md files, plain-text design-system documents extracted from real websites that AI coding agents read to generate UI consistent with a given design language. Each file captures analyzed design patterns, tokens, and rules as markdown for high-quality interface generation.

A repository of Agent Skills for Google products and Google Cloud, spanning areas such as GKE, BigQuery, IAM, Cloud Run, ads, and analytics. Skills are markdown instruction files, installable through the skills command, that AI coding agents load to carry out tasks against Google technologies.

OpenClaude is an open-source, terminal-first coding-agent CLI that works across cloud and local model providers, including OpenAI-compatible APIs, Gemini, GitHub Models, Codex, and Ollama. It provides prompts, file and shell tools, agents, MCP, slash commands, streaming output, and a bundled VS Code extension.

Vibe-Trading is a Python personal trading agent that equips an LLM agent with comprehensive trading capabilities, including historical-data loaders, broker connectors, backtesting, and factor analysis. It supports multi-agent quantitative-finance workflows and exposes an API and MCP server.

Graphify is an AI coding-assistant skill that turns a folder of code, SQL schemas, scripts, docs, papers, images, or videos into a queryable knowledge graph. It combines tree-sitter parsing, GraphRAG, and Leiden clustering so assistants can query app code, database schema, and infrastructure together.

OpenAB is a lightweight, cloud-native Rust broker that bridges chat platforms such as Discord and Slack to any Agent Client Protocol-compatible coding CLI over stdio JSON-RPC. It stays a thin broker, converging platform adapters on a shared dispatcher and session pool before crossing one ACP boundary to the selected agent runtime.

Career-Ops turns any AI coding CLI into a job-search command center. It scans job portals, scores listings A-F across five dimensions, generates ATS-optimized tailored CVs, and tracks applications locally, running as a skill inside assistants such as Claude Code, Gemini, Codex, and OpenCode.

The MATLAB Agentic Toolkit gives AI coding agents trusted MATLAB capabilities by automatically installing the MATLAB MCP Server and providing curated skills. It helps agents write idiomatic code, generate and run tests, diagnose errors, and build apps while avoiding hallucinated toolbox functions.

Hivemind is a cloud-backed shared memory layer for AI coding agents that captures each session's prompts, tool calls, and responses as structured traces, then mines them for repeated patterns and codifies those into reusable skill files shared across every agent on a team.

solopreneur is a family of Claude Code plugins that give solo founders the workflows of a full engineering organization, including plan review, code review loops, and automated PR cycles, plus marketing, design, and platform-specific expert agents installable a la carte.

Nuwa is an AI agent skill built on the open Agent Skills protocol that distills how a specific person thinks, including their mental models, decision heuristics, and expression style, from just a name, running research, extraction, and validation across many compatible agent runtimes.

GBrain is a self-hostable knowledge and memory layer for AI agents that adds synthesis, graph traversal, and gap analysis on top of retrieval. It ingests sources such as meetings, emails, and messages, enriches people and companies, and wires into agents like Claude Code or Codex as a shared brain.

Palmier Pro is an open source, Swift-native macOS video editor built for AI, letting you and an agent generate and edit videos together inside the timeline. It integrates generative models for creating video and images and connects to agents through MCP.

A Claude Code skill that distills Professor Hung-yi Lee's machine-learning lectures into a 916-node knowledge graph and teaching-style guide, letting an AI assistant explain topics like attention or DeepSeek-R1 in his intuition-first structure, calibrated against a real interview with him.

Webwright is a minimal browser-agent framework where a coding agent uses a terminal to write and run Playwright scripts, treating web tasks as re-runnable code instead of step-by-step actions. It reports state-of-the-art results on long-horizon web benchmarks and ships as a Claude Code and Codex plugin.

A curated awesome list of thousands of community-built OpenClaw skills, sourced from the ClawHub registry and organized by category such as coding agents, browser automation, and DevOps, to help users discover and install skills, with notes on vetting sources for security.

Voicebox is an open-source, local-first AI voice studio for macOS, Windows, and Linux that clones voices, generates speech across seven TTS engines in 23 languages, dictates into any app with Whisper, and lets MCP-aware agents speak through a built-in REST and MCP server.

A Claude Code skill that turns many content sources such as WeChat articles, web pages, YouTube, podcasts, PDFs, EPUB, and Office files into podcasts, slide decks, mind maps, quizzes, and reports by fetching the content and generating outputs through Google NotebookLM.

A Claude Code skill for creating programmatic videos with the Remotion React framework. It uses a scene-based architecture with automatic timing, AI-powered text-to-speech via Edge TTS or MiniMax, and supports animations, subtitles, and music visualization.

An anti-detection headless browser server for AI agents, built on the Camoufox Firefox fork. It exposes a REST API with token-efficient accessibility snapshots and stable element refs, and supports proxies with GeoIP, session isolation, cookie import, and search macros for common sites.

An engineering framework for AI coding agents that persists specs, tasks, and project memory into your repository so agents follow your conventions. It runs a plan, implement, verify, and finish workflow with auto-injected context and supports multiple AI coding platforms.

An open-source world model for video generation that simulates high-fidelity, diverse environments from an input image and text prompt. It supports camera-pose and action control signals, long-term temporal consistency, and real-time interactivity, and provides public code and model weights.

A single CLAUDE.md guidelines file to improve Claude Code behavior, derived from Andrej Karpathy's observations on LLM coding pitfalls. It defines four principles (think before coding, simplicity first, surgical changes, and goal-driven execution) and installs as a plugin or per-project config.

Polars-native Python platform for quantitative alpha research and systematic trading. It provides 72 Rust-backed factor operators for time-series and cross-sectional computation, using a column-based panel model that keeps every symbol on a shared timestamp index, prevents look-ahead bias by construction, and normalizes missing values to a single null type.

A command-line tool that hooks into Claude Code's SessionEnd event to generate a receipt for each coding session. It breaks down spend by model along with token counts and can render the result as a styled HTML page, ASCII console output, or a printout on a compatible thermal receipt printer.

An open-source, Ghostty-based macOS terminal built for working with AI coding agents. It offers vertical and horizontal tabs, notification rings that highlight panes and tabs when agents need attention, a scriptable in-app browser, and a sidebar showing git branch, linked pull request status, working directory, and listening ports.

A personal dotfiles repository that stores the author's own environment configuration files. The repository provides no description or documentation beyond its title, and its primary listed language is GLSL.

Thaw is a menu bar management tool for macOS 26 and 27, forked from the Ice project to keep it maintained. Its primary function is hiding and showing menu bar items, and it aims to add further features to become a versatile menu bar utility. It is built with SwiftUI and installable via direct download or Homebrew.

Project Golem is a self-hosted AI operations hub with a web dashboard that combines chat, long-term memory, multi-agent collaboration, a skills system, and MCP tool extensions. It runs on Gemini Web, Ollama, or LM Studio backends and adds features such as a stock board, collaborative calendar, and text RPG.

DenchClaw is a managed framework built on OpenClaw for knowledge work, providing CRM automation and outreach agents through a local web UI. It is installed and run via an npx command-line bootstrap that provisions a dedicated OpenClaw gateway and profile, with optional daemonless and Docker operation.

A CLI self-evolution engine for AI agents that scans runtime logs and memory, selects reusable Gene and Capsule assets under the GEP protocol, and emits protocol-bound prompts plus auditable evolution events. It integrates with agent runtimes and runs fully offline.

A lightweight, self-hosted personal AI agent runtime with a browser WebUI and terminal interface. It connects to chat apps such as Telegram, Discord, and Slack, supports tools, memory, MCP, and model routing, and exposes a Python SDK and OpenAI-compatible API for automations.

A native Swift macOS menu-bar app that tracks usage of AI coding subscriptions, showing session and weekly limits, credits, and spend for providers like Claude, Codex, Copilot, and Cursor in one popover. It also offers a one-shot CLI and a loopback HTTP API.

A CLI and MCP server that lets AI agents control your real Chrome browser using your existing login state, reaching many websites through their human interface instead of APIs. It ships community adapters for dozens of platforms and general browser automation commands.

A lightweight, self-hosted AI assistant that runs each agent in its own isolated Docker container for security. It connects to messaging apps such as WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, and Discord, supports memory and scheduled jobs, and is built on Anthropic's Claude Agent SDK.

A Python terminal dashboard for Polymarket BTC 15-minute UP/DOWN prediction markets that aggregates Chainlink oracle, Binance, and Polymarket orderbook feeds, computes real-time indicators, logs each market to CSV, and includes backtesting tools for trading strategy research.

OMX (oh-my-codex) is a TypeScript command-line tool, installed via npm, that extends the Codex coding agent with hooks, agent teams, HUDs, custom prompts, workflows, and runtime help intended to scale as engineering tasks grow.

A Model Context Protocol server built on Node.js and TypeScript that queries Taiwan government e-procurement tenders by scraping the official site with Cheerio and Axios, handling Big5 and UTF-8 encoding and returning formatted listings with bid periods for AI assistants.

The official draw.io Model Context Protocol server that lets large language models create and open diagrams in the draw.io editor, offering an inline app server, an npm tool server, assistant plugins, and project-instruction approaches supporting XML, CSV, and Mermaid input.

A collection of small, composable, model-agnostic agent skills for real software engineering, installable through the npx skills installer onto coding agents, and designed to be easy to adapt and to integrate with GitHub, Linear, or local files for issue tracking.

A Chrome extension that imports web pages, Substack articles, podcasts, RSS feeds, and documentation sites into Google NotebookLM, merging multiple articles into single sources to work around its 50-source limit and rescuing failed imports.

A Python benchmarking tool that sweeps draft and target model combinations to find the fastest pairing for speculative decoding, measuring LLM inference throughput, saving results to JSON, and rendering them as interactive charts.

A curated, drop-in collection of production-ready Claude Code skills spanning coding, design, documents, media, research, and automation, with each skill kept in its own folder ready to install into a local skills directory.

A community-curated collection of real-life OpenClaw agent use cases across social media, content creation, and daily automation, each linking to a walkthrough for setting up the agent to accomplish a practical everyday task.

A Python command-line tool that automates the Xiaohongshu (RED) platform via the Chrome DevTools Protocol, supporting image and video publishing, multi-account management, headless operation, content search, comment posting, and analytics export.

A Claude Code skill for paid-media operations across twelve advertising platforms including Google, Meta, TikTok, and LinkedIn. It turns authorized account data into evidence-backed audits, campaign plans, creative briefs, and versioned JSON reports, staying read-only by default with live account changes gated behind approval, verification, and rollback controls.

A browser-based video editor for creating product demos and mockups. Users record their screen or upload footage, then add smooth zooms, device frames, 3D camera movement, custom backgrounds, and overlays before exporting up to 4K in MP4, WebM, or GIF. Rendering runs fully in the browser using FFmpeg.wasm and Three.js.

A Chinese-language tutorial collection covering three AI coding tools: Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Codex. It provides 39 structured guides plus a quick-reference card spanning installation, commands, MCP integration, hooks, subagents, and team workflows, aimed at beginners, developers, and enterprise or academic training.

An open-source image editing foundation model built on PyTorch diffusion methods. It supports instruction-based edits with strong identity consistency, multi-element fusion, portrait makeup, and text style reference, and ships LoRA training code, an optimized inference pipeline, ComfyUI nodes, and the REDEdit-Bench evaluation set.

A curated awesome list of resources for software design at the code and component level. It gathers implementation and design patterns, API and interface design guides, architecture decision record templates and tooling, documentation-as-code approaches, architecture verification techniques, operational case studies, and recommended books.

A Claude Code custom skill that turns Claude into a video prompt engineer for ByteDance's Seedance 2.0 platform. From a natural-language idea it generates structured, production-ready Chinese prompts covering text-to-video, reference control, timestamp storyboarding, sound design, and multi-segment stitching.

A template that turns a single Claude Code session into a structured game development studio, providing 49 specialized AI agents, 73 workflow skills, validation hooks, path-scoped coding rules, and document templates organized into a director, lead, and specialist hierarchy for solo AI-assisted game development.

A Python backtesting framework for prediction-market trading strategies on Polymarket, built as an extension to NautilusTrader with custom exchange adapters. It adds staged data loading, execution and fee modeling, statistical optimization, and extensive single and multi-market performance charting.

A single-binary Rust agent runtime for self-hosted personal AI assistants. It connects to around twenty LLM providers, reaches users across thirty-plus channels such as Discord, Telegram, email, and CLI, and acts through shell, browser, HTTP, and hardware tools with security policies, sandboxing, and tool receipts.

An agentic IDE and orchestrator for running fleets of parallel AI coding agents such as Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor in isolated git worktrees. A local daemon and desktop app supervise sessions, terminals, and pull requests, routing CI failures, review comments, and merge conflicts back to the right agent.

A local LLM inference server for Apple Silicon that serves LLMs, vision-language models, embeddings, and rerankers with continuous batching and a tiered hot RAM and cold SSD KV cache. It exposes OpenAI- and Anthropic-compatible APIs and is managed from a native macOS menu bar app and web admin dashboard.

A PostgreSQL extension that adds durable, fault-tolerant execution to SQL functions. Workflows are defined in SQL with composable operators and checkpointed step by step, so they resume from the last checkpoint after crashes, restarts, or failed steps without external queues, workers, or orchestrators.

A command-line tool that replaces localhost port numbers with stable, named local development URLs, for both human developers and agents. It runs a local reverse proxy with automatic HTTPS and HTTP/2, assigns application ports automatically, and supports monorepos with per-package hostnames.

A terminal tool that right-sizes LLM models to a machine’s RAM, CPU, and GPU. It detects the hardware, scores each model across quality, speed, fit, and context, and reports which ones will run well. Ships with an interactive TUI and a classic CLI, and supports local runtimes such as Ollama, llama.cpp, and MLX.

An economic benchmark that evaluates AI agents as coworkers by having them complete professional tasks from the GDPVal dataset across dozens of occupations. Each agent starts with a small balance, pays for its own token usage, and must stay solvent, measuring work quality, cost efficiency, and long-term survival rather than only technical scores.

Open-source observability platform for traces, logs, and metrics, built on OpenTelemetry and ClickHouse. A TypeScript monorepo pairs a single-page dashboard with an Effect-based backend API, an OTLP ingest gateway, and an alerting worker.

An experimental autonomous AI agent that runs a continuous think-act-observe loop with access to a Linux sandbox, shell, file I/O, and on-chain transactions. It can modify its own source, spawn child agents, and manages compute through a credit-based survival model.

A standalone Go HTTP server that gives AI agents control over headless Chrome via the Chrome DevTools Protocol. It offers a token-efficient HTTP API, an MCP server, stealth injection, multi-instance orchestration, and a dashboard, defaulting to a local-first security posture.

A collection of packaged engineering skills and slash commands that guide AI coding agents through the development lifecycle: spec, plan, build, test, review, and ship. Skills encode workflows and quality gates and install into Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and other agents.

A config-first, file-aware dashboard for monitoring multiple AI agents. It reads OpenClaw agent configuration and each agent's workspace Markdown files to derive agent identity and activity status, and ships as a single HTML file with a minimal Node.js server.

A lightweight self-hosted web dashboard for monitoring OpenClaw bots, agents, models, and sessions at a glance. It reads local OpenClaw configuration and session files with no database, presenting bot overviews, model lists, session and token statistics, gateway health, configurable alerts, and an animated pixel-art office view.

Edda is a local-first, hash-chained memory ledger for AI coding sessions that captures each decision and its rationale and injects prior decisions into the next session. It works with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and other MCP clients through a CLI and hooks, with a deterministic core and optional LLM assist for session digests and decision extraction.

OpenAlice is a local trading workspace that turns coding agents into trading agents by giving them workspaces, markdown issues, tracked entities, an inbox, market data tools, and approval-gated trading primitives across equities, crypto, commodities, forex, and macro. It is experimental software and is not intended for live trading with real funds.

OpenHuman is a local-first personal AI system that builds a memory of a user's life, orchestrates fleets of agents and workflows, and performs deep research. Described by its authors as a personal AI brain that is simple and powerful, it is in early beta and under active development.

Lossless Claw is a Lossless Context Management plugin for the OpenClaw coding agent. It replaces the built-in sliding-window compaction with a DAG-based summarization system that persists every message in a SQLite database while keeping the active context within the model token limits, and it ships with a session migration CLI.

A local-first Go tool to browse, search, and track token usage and costs across AI coding agent sessions from Claude Code, Codex, and more than 20 other agents. Ships as one binary with a CLI, web UI, desktop app, and local SQLite storage.

A collection of portable agent skills that guide AI coding assistants such as Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor to build stronger frontend interfaces, improving layout, typography, motion, and spacing, plus image-generation skills for reference boards.

An OpenClaw plugin implementing the Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol v0.3.0 as a gateway for bidirectional communication between AI agents across servers, with JSON-RPC, REST, and gRPC transports, automatic peer discovery, adaptive routing, and circuit-breaker resilience.

A self-hosted, AI-powered news aggregator that fetches, deduplicates, scores, and enriches stories from Hacker News, Reddit, Telegram, RSS, and GitHub, then generates daily English and Chinese briefings delivered via a static site, email, and chat webhooks.

A stealth Chromium browser with source-level C++ fingerprint patches offered as a drop-in Playwright and Puppeteer replacement for Python, JavaScript, and .NET, aiming to pass automated bot-detection and CAPTCHA checks during browser automation and web scraping.

ClawFeed is a self-hosted, AI-powered news digest that aggregates Twitter, RSS, Hacker News, Reddit, and other sources into structured 4-hourly, daily, weekly, and monthly summaries, with a web dashboard, RSS and JSON feed output, and multi-user Google OAuth support.

Edict is a multi-agent AI orchestration system for the OpenClaw platform, organizing specialized agents after the ancient Chinese Three Departments and Six Ministries structure. It adds an institutional review layer, a real-time Kanban dashboard, per-agent model configuration, and full task audit trails.

Context Mode is an MCP server that optimizes the context window of AI coding agents. It sandboxes large tool output for up to 98 percent reduction, persists session memory in SQLite with full-text search for continuity across compaction, and enforces tool routing through hooks across 17 platforms.

PaintFE is a fast, lightweight open-source raster image editor built in Rust and shipped as a single portable executable. It offers layers, 25 blend modes, GPU-accelerated filters, brushes, selection tools, mesh warp, animated GIF and APNG export, an embedded Rhai scripting engine, a CLI batch mode, and local ONNX background removal.

Claude for Financial Services provides reference AI agents, skills, and MCP data connectors for investment banking, equity research, private equity, and wealth management workflows. Each agent installs as a Claude Cowork or Claude Code plugin and can also deploy through the Claude Managed Agents API.

A Python command-line tool that gives AI agents the ability to read and search platforms including Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, and XiaoHongShu, handling authentication and access blocks so agents can retrieve web content and transcripts without paid platform APIs.

A TypeScript web application that visualizes Taiwan's real-time transportation and open data on interactive 3D maps, rendering live flights, ships, trains, and buses alongside station, weather, population, and disaster-alert layers with playback of historical trails.

A code intelligence server that indexes source repositories into a persistent knowledge graph using tree-sitter parsing across many programming languages, exposing sub-millisecond structural queries to AI coding agents through the Model Context Protocol as a single dependency-free binary.

An open-source agent operating system built in Rust for running autonomous AI agents that work on schedules rather than waiting for prompts, distributed as a single binary with a multi-crate architecture for tasks such as monitoring, building knowledge graphs, and generating leads.

A Python local AI inference engine optimized for Apple Silicon that serves large language models behind an OpenAI- and Anthropic-compatible API, offering prompt caching, tool calling, and reasoning separation as a drop-in replacement for other local model runners.
